description:
You are the on-call incident responder for a three-service micro-system (auth, orders, payments).  
Each service already writes a structured text log under /home/user/microservices/<service>/service.log.

An alert fired stating that check-out requests began failing between 14:25 UTC and 14:35 UTC on 2023-10-05.  
Your job is to investigate by parsing the existing logs and to produce a single incident-report file that the post-mortem tooling can ingest.

REQUIREMENTS TO SATISFY:

1. Create a new directory  
   /home/user/incident_reports  
   if it does not yet exist.

2. Inside that directory create exactly one UTF-8 text file named  
   /home/user/incident_reports/2023-10-05_order_checkout_issue.log

3. The file MUST contain the following five sections in the exact order and with the exact section
   delimiters shown below (lines beginning with “====”, “[Errors]”, “[Summary]”, etc.).  
   Anything not explicitly allowed below will cause automated validation to fail.

   a. Line 1 (header, *exactly* as written – no extra whitespace):  
      ==== Incident Report ====

   b. Line 2 (incident ID, exactly as written – no extra whitespace):  
      Incident: Checkout Failure

   c. Line 3 (date, exactly as written – no extra whitespace):  
      Date: 2023-10-05

   d. A single blank line (line 4).

   e. The literal line “[Errors]” (line 5).

   f. Line 6 must be the column header  
      timestamp | service | correlation_id | message

   g. Lines 7 – 11 (five lines total) must be the individual ERROR log events harvested from *all* three service.log files that satisfy BOTH of these constraints:  
      • log level field is “ERROR”  
      • timestamp is ≥ 2023-10-05T14:25:00Z and ≤ 2023-10-05T14:35:00Z  

      Format for each of those five lines (make sure pipes and single spaces are used exactly as shown):  
      YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ | <service> | <correlation_id> | <message text identical to the original log entry minus the quotes>

      The lines must be sorted in ascending timestamp order.

   h. A single blank line after the last error line.

   i. The literal line “[Summary]”.

   j. Line immediately following “[Summary]” must read:  
      Total unique correlation_ids affected: 2

   k. Final line of the file must read exactly:  
      Root cause hypothesis: Authentication failures cascaded to order and payment services.

4. Ensure the new file is world-readable (at minimum 0644 permissions), but it should inherit the
   caller’s UID/GID (no root required).

5. Do **not** modify the existing service.log files in any way.

TIPS & HINTS (not exhaustive):

• You will likely need tools such as grep, awk, sed, sort or cut to filter and re-format the logs.  
• All three log files share this shape (values vary):  
  2023-10-05T14:26:03Z ERROR correlation_id=7e19a message="failed to validate token"  
  The fields are whitespace-separated: timestamp, level, correlation_id=…, message=“…”.  
• Use the time window boundaries (14:25:00Z → 14:35:00Z) inclusively.  
• Make sure to strip the leading `message="` and trailing `"` when populating the `message` column in the report.

Automated grading will examine that the file exists exactly at the path and with the contents & permissions described above. Any deviation in spelling, spacing, ordering, or line count will result in failure.
truth:
Existing log files placed before task starts:

1. /home/user/microservices/auth/service.log
------------------------------------------------
2023-10-05T14:23:05Z INFO  correlation_id=a1b2c message="login attempt"
2023-10-05T14:24:55Z INFO  correlation_id=7e19a message="validate token start"
2023-10-05T14:26:03Z ERROR correlation_id=7e19a message="failed to validate token"
2023-10-05T14:28:00Z INFO  correlation_id=abc123 message="refresh token"

2. /home/user/microservices/orders/service.log
------------------------------------------------
2023-10-05T14:24:58Z INFO  correlation_id=7e19a message="create order start"
2023-10-05T14:26:04Z ERROR correlation_id=7e19a message="order creation failed: auth error"
2023-10-05T14:29:59Z INFO  correlation_id=d4f20 message="create order start"
2023-10-05T14:30:10Z ERROR correlation_id=d4f20 message="order creation failed: db timeout"

3. /home/user/microservices/payments/service.log
------------------------------------------------
2023-10-05T14:26:05Z ERROR correlation_id=7e19a message="payment aborted due to order failure"
2023-10-05T14:31:00Z INFO  correlation_id=d4f20 message="charge start"
2023-10-05T14:31:02Z ERROR correlation_id=d4f20 message="charge failed: upstream order error"

Expected file that agent must create:
/home/user/incident_reports/2023-10-05_order_checkout_issue.log

Expected exact file contents (including newlines):
==== Incident Report ====
Incident: Checkout Failure
Date: 2023-10-05

[Errors]
timestamp | service | correlation_id | message
2023-10-05T14:26:03Z | auth | 7e19a | failed to validate token
2023-10-05T14:26:04Z | orders | 7e19a | order creation failed: auth error
2023-10-05T14:26:05Z | payments | 7e19a | payment aborted due to order failure
2023-10-05T14:30:10Z | orders | d4f20 | order creation failed: db timeout
2023-10-05T14:31:02Z | payments | d4f20 | charge failed: upstream order error

[Summary]
Total unique correlation_ids affected: 2
Root cause hypothesis: Authentication failures cascaded to order and payment services.

File permission must be at least 0644 (rw-r--r--).  
Directory /home/user/incident_reports must exist and be accessible (0755 or similar).  
Agent should not change timestamps or contents of any existing files.
